tasks.title: Allow auto-resolve to proceed when git merge reports conflicts
evidence: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/merge_runner.rs:226-233 shows resolve_conflicts calling `git merge` via `git_run` before checking conflicts.
crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/merge_runner.rs:331-339 shows `git_run` bailing on any non-zero exit code, which aborts before AI resolution when `git merge` exits with conflicts.
crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/merge_runner.rs:179-182 routes `ConflictPolicy::AutoResolve` into resolve_conflicts, so the non-zero merge exit blocks the intended path.
plan: Add a merge helper that treats `git merge` exit code 1 as ‘conflicts present’ and continues to conflict detection (while still erroring on other non-zero exits).
Add a test that creates a repo with a known conflict and asserts the auto-resolve path does not fail at the merge step.
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph merge_runner` and confirming the new test passes.

tasks.title: Validate queue/done JSON after AI conflict resolution before commit
evidence: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/merge_runner.rs:239-260 runs the merge-conflict prompt and then stages/commits without validating `.ralph/queue.json` or `.ralph/done.json`.
crates/ralph/src/queue.rs:106-131 provides `load_queue_with_repair_and_validate`, but it is not invoked in the merge conflict flow.
plan: After AI conflict resolution (and before `git add -A`), validate `.ralph/queue.json` and `.ralph/done.json` when present using `queue::load_queue_with_repair_and_validate` (or `validate_queue_set`).
Return a clear error if validation fails so bad queue merges never get committed; add tests covering invalid JSON and invalid task semantics.
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph merge_runner` and confirming validation failures are caught.

tasks.title: Sync parallel workspaces using configured queue/done paths
evidence: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/sync.rs:21-31 hard-codes `.ralph/queue.json` and `.ralph/done.json` for workspace sync.
crates/ralph/src/contracts/config.rs:67-72 documents configurable `queue.file` and `queue.done_file`, which are ignored by the sync implementation.
plan: Update `sync_ralph_state` to accept resolved queue/done paths (or a `Resolved` reference) and sync those files instead of hard-coded paths.
Update `run_loop_parallel` call sites and extend sync tests to cover custom queue/done paths from config.
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph sync_ralph_state` and confirming the custom-path test passes.

tasks.title: Prune stale parallel tasks when stored PID is no longer running
evidence: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs:136-145 only drops tasks_in_flight when the workspace path is missing; PID liveness is not checked.
crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/state.rs:87-101 stores `pid` in `ParallelTaskRecord`, but it is never used to detect dead workers.
plan: Add a cross-platform PID liveness check and use it when retaining `tasks_in_flight` so dead workers are pruned even if the workspace still exists.
Add tests that create a task record with a finished child PID and confirm it is dropped on startup.
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph parallel` and confirming the new test passes.

tasks.title: Ensure parallel loop cleans up workers on non-interrupt errors
evidence: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs:252-271 and 310-329 use `?` inside the main loop, so any error exits immediately.
crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs:355-380 shows cleanup (terminate_workers, workspace removal) only occurs when `interrupted` is true.
plan: Introduce a guard (e.g., scopeguard) that terminates workers, stops the merge runner, and persists state on any early return/error.
Add a test that injects a failure (e.g., force `sync_ralph_state` to fail) and asserts workers are terminated and state is cleaned.
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph parallel` and confirming the cleanup-on-error test passes.

tasks.title: Prevent duplicate task selection across concurrent run loops
evidence: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/mod.rs:359-371 runs parallel workers with `QueueLockMode::Skip`, so they do not hold the queue lock.
crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/worker.rs:46-65 selects tasks under a short-lived lock but never marks them `Doing` in the main queue.
crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs:240-272 shows tasks are spawned without updating the main queue, so a concurrent run can reselect the same task.
plan: Add a parallel run lock (dir lock) held for the duration of the parallel loop OR mark selected tasks as `Doing` in the main queue before spawning workers.
Add a test that starts a second run while a parallel loop is active and verifies it either fails fast or skips in-flight tasks.
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph parallel` and confirming concurrency protection works.

tasks.title: Avoid copying heavy gitignored directories into each workspace
evidence: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/sync.rs:109-170 copies every gitignored path recursively into each workspace (no size or allow/deny filtering).
crates/ralph/src/git/status.rs:80-104 shows ignored paths are sourced from `git ls-files -i -o --exclude-standard`, which includes large directories like `target/` or `node_modules/` if ignored.
plan: Add a denylist or size threshold for ignored paths (default-skip `target/`, `node_modules/`, `.cache/`), while still copying critical files like `.env`.
Add tests to ensure `.env` is synced but heavy ignored directories are skipped by default.
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph sync_ralph_state` and confirming the new skip behavior.

tasks.title: Add queue-aware guidance to merge conflict prompt
evidence: crates/ralph/assets/prompts/merge_conflicts.md:1-13 contains only generic merge instructions and does not mention preserving queue ordering or validating `.ralph/queue.json`/`.ralph/done.json`.
plan: Extend merge-conflict prompt generation to append queue-specific instructions when conflict files include `.ralph/queue.json` or `.ralph/done.json`.
Add a test that verifies the prompt includes queue-preservation guidance when those files are conflicted.
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph promptflow` and confirming the prompt test passes.

tasks.title: Respect max_tasks when resuming with in-flight parallel state
evidence: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs:223-224 initializes `tasks_started` to 0 regardless of existing `state_file.tasks_in_flight`.
crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs:241-243 uses `tasks_started < opts.max_tasks` to decide whether to spawn new workers, which can exceed the intended cap after resume.
plan: Initialize `tasks_started` from the number of existing in-flight tasks in `state_file` so the max-tasks cap accounts for resumed work.
Add a test that loads a state file with in-flight tasks and asserts no additional tasks are spawned when `max_tasks` would be exceeded.
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph parallel` and confirming the max_tasks test passes.

tasks.title: Reconcile PR status to avoid permanently excluded tasks
evidence: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/worker.rs:80-82 excludes any task with a PR record where `merged` is false.
crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs:298 and 425 are the only call sites for `mark_pr_merged`, so manual merges/closures never update state.
crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/state.rs:78-81 only flips `merged` when invoked; there is no PR status refresh on startup.
plan: On parallel loop start, refresh PR status for unmerged records (via `gh pr view` or `pr_merge_status`) and mark merged/closed PRs in `state.json`.
Add tests using a stubbed `gh` CLI to simulate merged/closed PRs and assert tasks are no longer excluded.
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph parallel` and confirming PR reconciliation tests pass.
